raw.objective: “to quantify important covariates influencing the unbound phenytoin fraction in children in routine clinical practice and to propose an equation for calculating the covariates-adjusted total phenytoin concentration”
raw.covariates: Tested: triglycerides, urea, VPA, CBZ albumin, bilirubin, creatinine Included: albumin, urea, VPA
raw.observations: The proposed model allows the prediction of free PHT concentrations to a fairly accurate degree, thereby side-stepping the need for separate measurements of free PHT
raw.equations: Pfu=theta1-theta2*(ALB-42)+theta3*(UREA-7)+theta4*VPA UPC=Pfu*TPC Pfu is percentage (%) of free unbound phenytoin UPC is unbound phenytoin concentration (mg/L) TPC is total phenytoin concentration (mg/L)

extraction_target.title: The quantitative effect of serum albumin, serum urea, and valproic acid on unbound phenytoin concentrations in children.
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to quantify important covariates influencing the unbound phenytoin fraction in children in routine clinical practice and to propose an equation for calculating the covariates-adjusted total phenytoin concentration”

covariates.raw: Tested: triglycerides, urea, VPA, CBZ albumin, bilirubin, creatinine Included: albumin, urea, VPA

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
